sections.paragraph:
[“properly it would seem “to pluck”], to prune, especially the vine, Lev 25:3, Lev 25:4. Hence מְזַמֶּרֶת snuffers. (Arab. زبر to prune a vine, the letters מ and ב being interchanged).

Niphal pass. Isa 5:6.

Piel זִמֵּר-

(1) to sing, properly (as has been well observed by Albert Schultens and Bishop Lowth), to cut off the discourse or sentence, or song; to express a song divided according to rhythmical numbers, (compare قَِريضُ a song, properly a discourse divided, from قرض to cut, to cut off. Arab. زَمَرَ I. and II.; Syr. ܙܡܰܪ and ܙܰܡܪܶ; Eth. Conj. II. id.). Followed by a dative of the person whom the song celebrates, Jud. 5:3 Psa 9:12 30:5 47:7 and an acc. Psa 47:7 66:2 68:5 , 33 Psa 68:33.

(2) to play on a musical instrument [or to sing so accompanied], ψάλλειν. Psa 33:2 71:22 .

(3) to dance (Arabic زمر), which is also done according to rhythmical numbers, and is connected with singing and music (comp. צָחַק and שָׂחַק). Hence זֶמֶר. [It may be questioned whether זִמֵּר ever really meant to dance; this signification seems to be merely imagined in order to connect זֶמֶר with its root.]

Derivatives, זָמִיר, זְמִיר, זְמֹורָה, מִזְמֹור, מַזְמֵרָה, מְזַמֶּרֶת and also those which immediately follow.

זְמָר [emph. זְמָרָא] m. Chald. music of instruments, Dan 3:5, , 10 , 15 .
